parsed_review.summary: CP00-712 is a descriptor-only RP23 External Integrations II finance-integrations pack (P06 Claude-review/closeout tail + P07 scope/contract/type/impl/workflow/permission head, 150 units, RP23.P06.M09.S03-RP23.P07.M05.S04, risk C). Independent read-only verification supports the pending production_ready determination: (1) the CP712 binding (registry.js:307) matches the baseline, contract current_pack/latest_pack, manifest, and README; (2) I recomputed the deliverable distribution from included_units and it equals implementation 23 / security_audit 19 / ui 8 / claude_review 5 / test 16 / failure_recovery 64 / hermes_evidence 10 / fixture 5 = 150, matching the prompt; (3) descriptor/case-set generation is descriptor-only with a comprehensive no-write attestation, and validators (validators.js:8) reject sensitive payloads, runtime opening, cross-tenant access, audit-body exposure, and real data while requiring human-final-approval and forbidding any Claude-final-approval or enterprise-trust claim; (4) Hermes is confined to deterministic evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false) and is not embedded in product runtime; (5) tests cover CP712 binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and contract projection (model.test.js:52-54); (6) the contract latest_projection is CP712 with validation.valid=true. The pack is honest about its state (manifest/inspection/adjudication report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status pending_review), the queue confirms CP00-712 as live latest with CP00-713 next, and CP178+ review-evidence hardening holds (readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON; no auth failure, malformed JSON, or tool-call-shaped output; no prior invalid review attempts). Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer. blocks_goal_closeout=false because the pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 documents that recorded validation commands were not re-executed in this read-only pass.

findings.title: Validation/test results taken from recorded evidence; not re-executed in read-only review
findings.details: This hardened review is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash), so the 24 commands recorded in command-evidence.json (npm test, npm run validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validate, node --test, git diff --check) could not be re-run here. All are recorded with exit_code 0, and readiness-report.json shows the auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (1434 bytes). I independently corroborated these claims by static inspection: the CP712 binding matches the baseline; the deliverable distribution recomputed from included_units equals the prompt's distribution (sum 150); validators enforce descriptor_only/no-sensitive-payload/no-runtime/deny-over-allow/no-cross-tenant/no-audit-body/synthetic-only/human-final-approval; and contracts/external-integrations-ii-contract.json reports latest_projection=CP712 with validation.valid=true. Residual risk is limited to the command exit codes themselves not being re-executed in this pass.
findings.recommendation: Rely on the deterministic control-plane (CI) re-execution of the recorded validation commands as the binding pass/fail signal; no pack change is required.
